webhooks.name: mraycluster.ray.openshift.ai
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources on create/update to inject an OAuth proxy sidecar container for Ray Dashboard authentication and mTLS init containers, volumes, and environment variables for encrypted inter-node communication. The validating webhook ensures these injected components are not tampered with and prevents enabling the native ingress.

webhooks.name: vraycluster.ray.openshift.ai
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources on create/update to inject an OAuth proxy sidecar container for Ray Dashboard authentication and mTLS init containers, volumes, and environment variables for encrypted inter-node communication. The validating webhook ensures these injected components are not tampered with and prevents enabling the native ingress.

webhooks.name: clusterservingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update to enforce model format priority uniqueness across runtimes sharing the same protocol version, ensure consistent priorities for the same model format within a single runtime, and validate multi-node WorkerSpec constraints (single container, valid GPU types, valid pipeline/tensor parallel sizes, no removal of existing workerSpec, and blocked environment variables).

webhooks.name: inferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.pod-mutator
webhooks.purpose: Mutates Pods belonging to KServe InferenceServices at creation time, injecting storage initializer init-containers for model downloading, cloud-storage credential/secret volume mounts, inference-logging and request-batching agent sidecars, metrics-aggregator sidecars, GKE GPU accelerator node selectors, and optionally OCI modelcar support.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.defaulter
webhooks.purpose: Mutating defaulter for LLMInferenceService resources that applies default values and sets local model cache labels/annotations. It reads the inference service configmap and, if local model caching is enabled, matches the service's model URI against cluster-scoped LocalModelCache and namespace-scoped LocalModelNamespaceCache resources to attach the appropriate PVC name and source URI metadata, with namespace-scoped caches taking precedence.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for LLMInferenceService v1alpha1 resources on create and update. It enforces router cross-field constraints (e.g., HTTPRoute refs vs inline spec mutual exclusivity, managed vs user-defined gateway compatibility), parallelism constraints (worker requires parallelism, pipeline and data parallelism cannot coexist, data/dataLocal must be paired, positive values), scheduler config validity (replicas > 0, inline xor ref), workload scaling rules (delegated to v1alpha2 hub validators), and immutability of total parallelism size on updates.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.defaulter
webhooks.purpose: Mutating defaulter for LLMInferenceService resources that applies default values and sets local model cache labels/annotations. It reads the inference service configmap and, if local model caching is enabled, matches the service's model URI against cluster-scoped LocalModelCache and namespace-scoped LocalModelNamespaceCache resources to attach the appropriate PVC name and source URI metadata, with namespace-scoped caches taking precedence.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validating admission webhook for LLMInferenceService v1alpha2 resources on create and update. It enforces router/gateway cross-field consistency, parallelism constraints (pipeline vs data parallelism mutual exclusivity, required companion fields), immutability of total parallelism size on updates, scheduler config completeness, LoRA adapter name uniqueness and safety, autoscaling rules (replica bounds, HPA/KEDA mutual exclusivity, WVA requirement, variantCost format, KEDA idleReplicaCount constraints), and actuator backend consistency between decode and prefill workloads.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update, rejecting specs that use the forbidden baseRefs field or specify non-positive scheduler replicas, and emitting warnings when well-known (built-in) configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update, rejecting configs that use the forbidden baseRefs field (recursive refs are disabled) or specify non-positive scheduler replicas, and emits warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update to enforce model format priority uniqueness across runtimes sharing the same protocol version, ensure consistent priorities for the same model format within a single runtime, and validate multi-node WorkerSpec constraints (single container, valid GPU types, valid pipeline/tensor parallel sizes, no removal of existing workerSpec, and blocked environment variables).

webhooks.name: clusterservingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update to ensure model format priorities are consistent (no duplicate priorities across runtimes for the same model format, and same priorities within a single runtime for the same format), and validates multi-node WorkerSpec constraints including GPU resource types, parallel size values, blocked environment variables, and disallowed structural changes like removing an existing WorkerSpec.

webhooks.name: inferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.pod-mutator
webhooks.purpose: Mutates pods managed by KServe InferenceServices on creation by injecting storage initializer containers (for model downloading with appropriate credentials), inference agent sidecars (for request logging and batching), GKE accelerator node selectors, metrics aggregator sidecars, and optionally modelcar init containers, ensuring pods are fully configured before they run.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.defaulter
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that defaults LLMInferenceService resources by matching their model URI against existing LocalModelNamespaceCache (namespace-scoped, higher priority) and LocalModelCache (cluster-scoped) resources, then setting labels and annotations (local model name, PVC name, source URI) so the controller can mount the correct local model PVC for serving. If local model caching is disabled via config or annotation, it removes any previously set local model metadata.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceService v1alpha1 resources on create and update, enforcing router/gateway cross-field constraints (e.g., HTTPRoute refs vs inline spec exclusivity, managed-gateway compatibility), parallelism rules (worker requires parallelism, data and pipeline parallelism mutual exclusivity, data/dataLocal co-requirement, positive values), scheduler config consistency (replicas > 0, exactly one of inline or ref), scaling rules (delegated to v1alpha2), and immutability of total parallelism size on updates.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.defaulter
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that defaults LLMInferenceService resources by matching their model URI against existing LocalModelNamespaceCache (namespace-scoped, higher priority) and LocalModelCache (cluster-scoped) resources, then setting labels and annotations (local model name, PVC name, source URI) so the controller can mount the correct local model PVC for serving. If local model caching is disabled via config or annotation, it removes any previously set local model metadata.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validating admission webhook for LLMInferenceService v1alpha2 resources that enforces structural and semantic constraints on create and update operations, including router/HTTPRoute cross-field consistency, parallelism configuration and immutability, scheduler config validity, autoscaling rules (replica bounds, WVA/HPA/KEDA mutual exclusivity, actuator consistency between decode and prefill workloads, KEDA controller-owned field restrictions), LoRA adapter naming uniqueness and validity, managed DRA annotation completeness and format, and confidential model spec validation.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update by forbidding the baseRefs field (recursive refs are disabled), ensuring scheduler replicas are greater than zero, and running any injected validation function. It also emits warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update by rejecting specs that contain baseRefs (recursive refs are disabled), ensuring scheduler replicas are greater than zero, and running any injected validation function. It also emits warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update to ensure model format priorities are consistent (no duplicate priorities across runtimes for the same model format, and same priorities within a single runtime for the same format), and validates multi-node WorkerSpec constraints including GPU resource types, parallel size values, blocked environment variables, and disallowed structural changes like removing an existing WorkerSpec.

webhooks.name: notebooks-validation.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates updates to Notebook resources, specifically preventing removal of the MLflow instance annotation while a notebook is running. This guards against a broken state where MLflow environment variables persist in the pod but the associated RoleBinding has already been deleted, leaving the MLflow client with credentials but no permissions.

webhooks.name: notebooks.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook for Kubeflow Notebook resources that injects operational sidecars (kube-rbac-proxy for authentication), mounts trusted CA certificate bundles, resolves container images from OpenShift ImageStreams, injects cluster-wide proxy environment variables, syncs and mounts pipeline runtime ConfigMaps and Elyra/DSPA pipeline configuration secrets, configures MLflow tracking environment variables, manages Feast config volumes, and blocks pod-template-changing mutations on running notebooks to prevent unintended restarts (marking them as update-pending instead).

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources on create/update to enforce platform-specific network security. On OpenShift (detected via RESTMapper lookup of the Route CRD), it sets the secure-trusted-network annotation to true and forcibly disables enableIngress on the head group spec to enforce Gateway API-only access, preventing direct Route/Ingress creation and ensuring centralized authentication via Gateway.

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources on create/update to enforce platform-specific network security. On OpenShift (detected via RESTMapper lookup of the Route CRD), it sets the secure-trusted-network annotation to true and forcibly disables enableIngress on the head group spec to enforce Gateway API-only access, preventing direct Route/Ingress creation and ensuring centralized authentication via Gateway.

webhooks.name: vraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates RayCluster resources on create and update, ensuring the cluster name matches a DNS-compatible lowercase alphanumeric pattern and that all worker group names within the spec are unique.

webhooks.name: vcohort.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates Cohort resources on create and update by checking the spec's fair sharing configuration and resource groups. It ensures cohort definitions conform to schema rules (e.g., valid resource groups and fair sharing settings) but does not mutate objects or read any external cluster state.

webhooks.name: mmodelregistry.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook defaults ModelRegistry fields (service routes, empty DB cleanup, OAuth-to-kube-rbac-proxy migration, runtime image cleanup) and validating webhook enforces cluster-wide name uniqueness on create, ensures the CR namespace matches the configured registries namespace, and requires at least one database (Postgres or MySQL) configuration.

webhooks.name: vmodelregistry.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook defaults ModelRegistry fields (service routes, empty DB cleanup, OAuth-to-kube-rbac-proxy migration, runtime image cleanup) and validating webhook enforces cluster-wide name uniqueness on create, ensures the CR namespace matches the configured registries namespace, and requires at least one database (Postgres or MySQL) configuration.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.modelmesh-webhook-server.default
webhooks.purpose: Validates autoscaler-related annotations on multi-model ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources during create/update, ensuring the autoscaler class is a supported type (e.g. HPA), metrics are valid, target utilization percentage is an integer between 1-100, min/max replica bounds are consistent, and that explicit replicas are not set alongside an enabled autoscaler.

webhooks.name: vaitenant.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook that enforces AITenant resources are only created in a specific configured infrastructure namespace. It rejects creation requests where the AITenant's namespace does not match the pre-configured AITenantNamespace, ensuring all tenants are co-located in the designated namespace.

webhooks.name: vmaasauthpolicy.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook that rejects creation of MaaSAuthPolicy resources in namespaces that have not been initialized as MaaS tenants. On create, it lists Tenant CRs in the target namespace and denies the request if none exist, ensuring proper tenant setup before auth policies can be configured.

webhooks.name: vmaassubscription.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook that rejects creation of MaaSSubscription resources in namespaces that have not been initialized as MaaS tenants. On create, it delegates to TenantNamespaceValidator which lists Tenant CRs in the target namespace and denies admission if none exist, ensuring proper tenant setup before subscriptions can be created.

webhooks.name: minferencegraph-v1alpha1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating defaulting (currently a no-op) and validating admission control for KServe InferenceGraph v1alpha1 resources. The validator enforces that every step's serviceURL is a well-formed cluster-internal address whose target namespace matches the InferenceGraph's own namespace, rejecting cross-namespace references and public hostnames to prevent credential leakage.

webhooks.name: minferenceservice-v1beta1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates and defaults KServe InferenceService resources. On creation, it enforces a name-length constraint and prevents InferenceServices from being created in the protected application namespace (determined by reading the DSCInitialization CR). The mutating defaulter is currently a no-op placeholder.

webhooks.name: mutating.pod.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates incoming Pods that have RAY_USE_TLS enabled on their kserve-container or worker-container by injecting TLS-related volumes, volume mounts, and an init container that copies Ray TLS certificates from a shared Secret into the pod, enabling mTLS communication between Ray cluster nodes used for distributed inference serving.

webhooks.name: validating.isvc.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates and defaults KServe InferenceService resources. On creation, it enforces a name-length constraint and prevents InferenceServices from being created in the protected application namespace (determined by reading the DSCInitialization CR). The mutating defaulter is currently a no-op placeholder.

webhooks.name: validating.nim.account.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates NIM Account creation by enforcing a singleton constraint: only one Account resource is allowed per namespace. On create, it lists existing Accounts in the target namespace and rejects the request if one already exists; updates and deletes are always permitted.

webhooks.name: vinferencegraph-v1alpha1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating defaulting (currently a no-op) and validating admission control for KServe InferenceGraph v1alpha1 resources. The validator enforces that every step's serviceURL is a well-formed cluster-internal address whose target namespace matches the InferenceGraph's own namespace, rejecting cross-namespace references and public hostnames to prevent credential leakage.

webhooks.name: vogxserver.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for OGXServer create/update operations. It enforces that the distribution name is known in the operator's registry, that provider IDs are unique, that model resource provider references point to configured providers, and that adoption annotations do not reference the CR's own name (which would cause Deployment name conflicts). It also emits deprecation warnings for renamed distributions.

webhooks.name: mutate-pod.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook that intercepts Pod create/update requests in Spark job namespaces, looks up the owning SparkApplication by the pod's spark-app-name label, and defaults the pod spec with settings from the SparkApplication CR—including volumes, env vars, config maps, affinity, tolerations, node selectors, security contexts, GPU resources, sidecar/init containers, ports, owner references, memory limits, host networking, and scheduling options—differentiating between driver and executor pods.

webhooks.name: mutate-sparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default values on SparkApplication resources during create and update operations. It only applies defaults (via the operator's webhook scheme) when the application is in a 'New' or 'Invalidating' state, leaving already-running or completed applications unchanged.

webhooks.name: validate-scheduledsparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates ScheduledSparkApplication resources on create and update by ensuring the metadata.name is a valid DNS-1035 label, preventing downstream failures when the operator later creates SparkApplication and Service resources that derive names from it. The core validation logic is otherwise a placeholder (TODO).

webhooks.name: validate-sparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: A mutating admission webhook defaulter for ScheduledSparkApplication resources. Currently a no-op placeholder that logs when a ScheduledSparkApplication is created or updated, but does not yet set any default values on the resource.

webhooks.name: dscinitialization-v2-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Enforces singleton semantics for DSCInitialization v2 resources by denying creation when one already exists, and prevents deletion of DSCInitialization while any DataScienceCluster object still exists in the cluster.

webhooks.name: connection-notebook.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for Kubeflow Notebook CRs that validates and injects 'connection' secrets. When a Notebook is created or updated with the opendatahub.io/connections annotation (comma-separated namespace/name secret references), it verifies each referenced Secret exists, checks the requesting user has 'get' permission on those Secrets via SubjectAccessReview, then mutates the notebook's first container by adding or removing the secrets as envFrom secretRef entries.

webhooks.name: dscinitialization-v1-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DSCInitialization v1 resources as singletons: on create, denies if another DSCInitialization instance already exists; on delete, denies if any DataScienceCluster object still exists, preventing removal of the initialization resource while the cluster is active.

webhooks.name: podmonitor-injector.opendatahub.io
webhooks.enable_condition: MonitoringServiceName component enabled OR MonitoringServiceName component enabled
webhooks.purpose: Mutates PodMonitor and ServiceMonitor resources on create/update by injecting the monitoring.opendatahub.io/scrape=true label when the resource's namespace is labeled for ODH monitoring and the cluster-scoped Monitoring CR exists, enabling Prometheus to discover these monitors automatically.

webhooks.name: hardwareprofile-notebook-injector.opendatahub.io
webhooks.enable_condition: Kserve component enabled OR Workbenches component enabled
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook that intercepts create/update of Notebooks, InferenceServices, and LLMInferenceServices to inject hardware profile settings (resource requests/limits, node selectors, tolerations) from a referenced HardwareProfile CR into the workload's pod spec. It also handles profile switching (clearing old settings before applying new ones) and profile removal (cleaning up previously injected settings when the annotation is removed).

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v2-defaulter.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default field values on DataScienceCluster v2 resources during create/update. Specifically, it defaults ModelRegistry.RegistriesNamespace to the operator's built-in default when ModelRegistry is Managed and the field is empty, and defaults Kserve.NIM.ManagementState to Managed when Kserve is Managed and the NIM state is unset.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v2-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DataScienceCluster v2 resources on create and update. On create, enforces a singleton constraint by listing existing DataScienceCluster instances and denying if one already exists, and rejects any DSC where Kueue's managementState is set to Managed. On update, only the Kueue managementState check is applied. Deletions and other operations are always allowed.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v1-defaulter.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default field values on DataScienceCluster v1 resources during create/update. Specifically, it defaults ModelRegistry.RegistriesNamespace to the operator's built-in default when ModelRegistry is Managed and the field is empty, and defaults Kserve.NIM.ManagementState to Managed when Kserve is Managed and NIM's state is unset.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v1-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DataScienceCluster v1 resources on create and update. On create, enforces singleton (only one DSC instance allowed) and rejects Kueue with ManagementState=Managed. On update, rejects Kueue Managed state and blocks v1 API modifications when v2-only components are currently Managed, preventing silent data loss during v1-to-v2 conversion where v2-only fields would revert to defaults.

webhooks.name: connection-isvc.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for KServe InferenceService resources that automatically injects model-serving connection details (storage URIs, image pull secrets, service accounts, S3 storage keys) into the ISVC spec based on a platform connection annotation, and cleans up or replaces those injected fields when the annotation is updated or removed.

webhooks.name: connection-llmisvc.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for LLMInferenceService resources (serving.kserve.io) that automatically injects model connection details based on a platform connection annotation. On create/update, it validates the referenced connection Secret, determines the protocol type (URI/HuggingFace, S3, or OCI), and patches the resource accordingly—setting spec.model.uri, injecting imagePullSecrets for OCI, or binding a ServiceAccount for S3 credentials.

webhooks.name: vworkspace.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating admission webhook for Workspace resources on create and update. It fetches the referenced WorkspaceKind to verify it exists, then validates that the Workspace's imageConfig and podConfig IDs match valid entries in that WorkspaceKind and that pod template metadata (labels/annotations) is well-formed.

webhooks.name: vworkspacekind.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating admission webhook for WorkspaceKind resources (kubeflow.org/v1beta1) that enforces structural correctness on create/update (pod metadata, environment variables, imageConfig/podConfig values including redirect cycle detection, port references, and default config validity) and prevents deletion when any Workspace still references the WorkspaceKind or its protection finalizer is present. On update it performs incremental validation, only re-checking changed or new config entries, and blocks removal of imageConfig/podConfig options that are actively in use by Workspaces.

webhooks.name: localmodelnamespacecache.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LocalModelNamespaceCache resources on create, update, and delete. On create/update, it ensures all referenced node groups exist; on delete, it prevents removal if the cache is still referenced by any InferenceService or LLMInferenceService via label matching.

webhooks.name: mresourceflavor.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for Kueue ResourceFlavor objects. The mutating webhook ensures a 'ResourceInUse' finalizer is added on creation, while the validating webhook checks that nodeLabels, nodeTaints (uniqueness by key+effect, valid label keys/values), and tolerations conform to Kubernetes conventions on create and update.

webhooks.name: vresourceflavor.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for Kueue ResourceFlavor objects. The mutating webhook ensures a 'ResourceInUse' finalizer is added on creation, while the validating webhook checks that nodeLabels, nodeTaints (uniqueness by key+effect, valid label keys/values), and tolerations conform to Kubernetes conventions on create and update.

webhooks.name: mclusterqueue.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Defaults and validates ClusterQueue resources. The mutating webhook adds a ResourceInUse finalizer on creation, while the validating webhook enforces correctness of resource groups (no duplicate resources/flavors, valid quantities), namespace selectors, admission check configuration (disallowing both AdmissionChecks and AdmissionChecksStrategy simultaneously), preemption policies, fair sharing settings, and borrowing/lending limits (must be nil when no cohort, lending must not exceed nominal quota).

webhooks.name: vclusterqueue.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Defaults and validates ClusterQueue resources. The mutating webhook adds a ResourceInUse finalizer on creation, while the validating webhook enforces correctness of resource groups (no duplicate resources/flavors, valid quantities), namespace selectors, admission check configuration (disallowing both AdmissionChecks and AdmissionChecksStrategy simultaneously), preemption policies, fair sharing settings, and borrowing/lending limits (must be nil when no cohort, lending must not exceed nominal quota).

webhooks.name: mworkload.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Defaults and validates Kueue Workload resources. The mutating webhook strips minCount from pod sets when the PartialAdmission feature gate is disabled, while the validating webhook enforces constraints on pod set definitions (e.g., reserved resource keys), admission assignments, reclaimable pod counts, admission check pod-set updates, and immutability of spec and admission fields once a quota reservation exists.

webhooks.name: vworkload.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Defaults and validates Kueue Workload resources. The mutating webhook strips minCount from pod sets when the PartialAdmission feature gate is disabled, while the validating webhook enforces constraints on pod set definitions (e.g., reserved resource keys), admission assignments, reclaimable pod counts, admission check pod-set updates, and immutability of spec and admission fields once a quota reservation exists.

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating admission webhook for KubeRay RayCluster resources integrated with Kueue. The mutating webhook applies a default local queue, suspends the cluster so Kueue controls admission, and sets the managedBy field; the validating webhook rejects in-tree autoscaling, limits worker groups to seven, forbids the reserved 'head' group name, validates topology-aware scheduling requests, and enforces standard Kueue job invariants on create and update.

webhooks.name: vraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating admission webhook for KubeRay RayCluster resources integrated with Kueue. The mutating webhook applies a default local queue, suspends the cluster so Kueue controls admission, and sets the managedBy field; the validating webhook rejects in-tree autoscaling, limits worker groups to seven, forbids the reserved 'head' group name, validates topology-aware scheduling requests, and enforces standard Kueue job invariants on create and update.

webhooks.name: mleaderworkerset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for LeaderWorkerSet resources managed by Kueue. The mutating webhook applies a default local queue, determines whether the workload should be suspended (by walking owner references for ancestor Kueue-managed jobs and optionally checking namespace labels), and annotates pod templates with suspension and priority class metadata. The validating webhook enforces queue name validity, topology-aware scheduling pod set requests, and on updates ensures immutability of the queue name label, priority class label, and pod template specs for Kueue-managed workloads.

webhooks.name: vleaderworkerset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for LeaderWorkerSet resources managed by Kueue. The mutating webhook applies a default local queue, determines whether the workload should be suspended (by walking owner references for ancestor Kueue-managed jobs and optionally checking namespace labels), and annotates pod templates with suspension and priority class metadata. The validating webhook enforces queue name validity, topology-aware scheduling pod set requests, and on updates ensures immutability of the queue name label, priority class label, and pod template specs for Kueue-managed workloads.

webhooks.name: mrayjob.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for RayJob resources managed by Kueue. The mutating hook defaults the local queue label, suspends jobs pending Kueue admission (walking owner references to avoid double-managing child jobs), and sets the managedBy field. The validating hook enforces that Kueue-managed RayJobs shut down their cluster after finishing, do not reference existing clusters or enable autoscaling, have at most 7 worker groups with no group named 'head', and carry valid topology-aware scheduling annotations.

webhooks.name: vrayjob.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating webhook for RayJob resources managed by Kueue. The mutating hook defaults the local queue label, suspends jobs pending Kueue admission (walking owner references to avoid double-managing child jobs), and sets the managedBy field. The validating hook enforces that Kueue-managed RayJobs shut down their cluster after finishing, do not reference existing clusters or enable autoscaling, have at most 7 worker groups with no group named 'head', and carry valid topology-aware scheduling annotations.

webhooks.name: mmpijob.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating and validating admission webhooks for Kubeflow MPIJob resources managed by Kueue. The mutating webhook applies default local queue names, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue (by checking the ownership chain and namespace selector), and sets the managedBy field. The validating webhook enforces Kueue queue-name constraints on create/update and validates Topology-Aware Scheduling annotations on each MPI replica spec's pod template.

webhooks.name: vmpijob.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Provides mutating and validating admission webhooks for Kubeflow MPIJob resources managed by Kueue. The mutating webhook applies default local queue names, suspends jobs that should be managed by Kueue (by checking the ownership chain and namespace selector), and sets the managedBy field. The validating webhook enforces Kueue queue-name constraints on create/update and validates Topology-Aware Scheduling annotations on each MPI replica spec's pod template.

webhooks.name: mjobset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates incoming JobSet resources by applying default local queue names, suspending jobs that should be managed by Kueue (checking ancestor ownership and namespace-level selectors), and setting the managedBy field; validates that queue-name labels are consistent, immutable fields are not changed on update, and topology-aware scheduling annotations on replicated job pod templates are well-formed.

webhooks.name: vjobset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates incoming JobSet resources by applying default local queue names, suspending jobs that should be managed by Kueue (checking ancestor ownership and namespace-level selectors), and setting the managedBy field; validates that queue-name labels are consistent, immutable fields are not changed on update, and topology-aware scheduling annotations on replicated job pod templates are well-formed.

webhooks.name: mstatefulset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates and validates StatefulSet objects for Kueue queue management: the mutating webhook applies a default local queue name, suspends the workload by annotating the pod template with pod-group labels and queue metadata, while the validating webhook enforces queue-name immutability when ready replicas exist, pod spec immutability under Kueue management, and restricts replica changes to scale-to-zero or scale-from-zero only.

webhooks.name: vstatefulset.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates and validates StatefulSet objects for Kueue queue management: the mutating webhook applies a default local queue name, suspends the workload by annotating the pod template with pod-group labels and queue metadata, while the validating webhook enforces queue-name immutability when ready replicas exist, pod spec immutability under Kueue management, and restricts replica changes to scale-to-zero or scale-from-zero only.

webhooks.name: validator.pytorchjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates PyTorchJob resources on create and update, enforcing naming conventions (DNS-1035), run policy constraints, elastic policy configuration rules (e.g., nProcPerNode deprecation), replica spec requirements (valid replica types of Master/Worker, exactly 1 master replica, at least one container named 'pytorch' with a defined image), and ensuring workers are configured when elastic policy is used.

webhooks.name: validator.xgboostjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates XGBoostJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is DNS-1035 compliant, a Master replica type with exactly 1 replica exists, only Master and Worker replica types are used, every container has an image defined, and at least one container is named 'xgboost'.

webhooks.name: validator.jaxjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates JAXJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, that jaxReplicaSpecs are present and only contain the 'Worker' replica type, that each replica spec has containers with images defined, and that at least one container is named 'jax'.

webhooks.name: validator.tfjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TFJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, each replica spec contains at least one container with a non-empty image and a container named 'tensorflow', and that no more than one Chief or Master replica type is defined.

webhooks.name: validator.paddlejob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates PaddleJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, replica types are limited to Master and Worker, each replica spec has containers with images defined, and at least one container is named 'paddle'. It also validates the RunPolicy and checks for immutability constraints on updates.

webhooks.name: validator.clustertrainingruntime.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates ClusterTrainingRuntime resources on create and update by ensuring their ReplicatedJobs spec is well-formed, and emits a deprecation warning on create if the runtime is labeled as deprecated per Kubeflow's runtime deprecation policy.

webhooks.name: validator.trainingruntime.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TrainingRuntime resources on creation by ensuring that replicated jobs labeled as ancestors (trainer, model-initializer, dataset-initializer) have exactly 1 replica and contain the required container name corresponding to their ancestor type.

webhooks.name: validator.trainjob.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TrainJob resources on create and update by resolving the TrainJob's runtimeRef to a registered runtime implementation and delegating validation to that runtime's ValidateObjects method, rejecting requests that reference unsupported runtimes.

webhooks.name: localmodelnamespacecache.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LocalModelNamespaceCache resources on create, update, and delete. On create/update, it ensures all referenced node groups exist; on delete, it prevents removal if any InferenceService or LLMInferenceService still references the cache.

webhooks.name: conversion.dscinitializations.dscinitialization.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Implements CRD conversion webhook for DSCInitialization, converting between the v1 (spoke) and v2 (hub) API versions by mapping spec fields (ApplicationsNamespace, Monitoring, TrustedCABundle, DevFlags) and status fields (Phase, Conditions, RelatedObjects, ErrorMessage, Release) bidirectionally so that clients using either API version can interact with the same stored resource.
